Question: The scientist who won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 is famous for an equation relating energy and mass. In what year was that scientist born? Answer with a number (a year).
A European national team won the men's FIFA World Cup in 2014. How many times had that same country won the men's World Cup in the years before 2014? Answer with a number.
Which film won the Academy Award for Best Picture at the ceremony held in 1998? Answer with the film title.
The Great Pyramid of Giza is located in a modern country. What is the capital city of that country? Answer with the city name.
The author who wrote the dystopian novel titled '1984' also wrote an allegorical novella set on a farm. What is the title of that novella? Answer with the title.
How many countries share a land border with BOTH France and Spain? Answer with a number.
How many years elapsed between the year the Berlin Wall fell and the year the Soviet Union was formally dissolved? Answer with a number.

Multiply the number of faces of a regular dodecahedron by the number of players from one team that are on the field at kickoff in a standard association football (soccer) match. What is the result? Answer with a number.
Andorra shares a land border with exactly two countries. Name them as a comma separated list in alphabetical order.
